weekly_summary: The client stayed engaged with daily check-ins throughout the week and showed a genuine recovery in energy and sleep by Day 8, but the week also included recurring bloating/acidity, several low-protein or skipped meals, and a high-stress Day 7 in which she briefly fell asleep during a work meeting after several nights of only 5-5.5 hours of sleep. Meal planning remains difficult to fit around her school-drop-off schedule.

findings.label: Nutrition adherence
findings.value: Adherence was inconsistent: protein and breakfast intake were low on multiple days, and habits like ACV and sprouts were missed despite reminders.

evidence.quote: Protein seems low in breakfast on some days
Forgot ACV today. Not yet in the habit.

findings.label: Exercise / steps
findings.value: Daily movement continued throughout the week (walking, stretching, household activity) with steps ranging roughly 4,500-8,000, but no structured workout beyond walking and light stretching.

findings.label: Sleep
findings.value: Sleep was short on most days (around 5-5.5 hours) and improved to about 8 hours only on Day 8, alongside a same-week episode of falling asleep briefly at work.

evidence.quote: Slept only around 5 hours last night
Slept better last night, around 8 hours

findings.label: Water intake
findings.value: Water intake was only explicitly logged on two of eight days (4L and 3.5L); most days have no reported figure.

findings.label: Symptoms / stress
findings.value: Acidity and bloating recurred across the week (Days 1, 2, 5, 6, 8), and Day 7 included a distinct high-stress episode with acute exhaustion at work.

evidence.quote: my head went down on the table and I actually slept for a few seconds

findings.label: Engagement level
findings.value: Engagement was generally high, with daily proactive updates, though the client missed one coach call on Day 7 during a stressful day at work.

evidence.quote: Sorry I missed your call. There was a stressful situation at work.

findings.label: Key barriers
findings.value: Main barriers were a hectic school-related morning schedule and insufficient time to plan or stock meals in advance.

evidence.quote: I am not getting enough time to plan meals
I still need to stock vegetables properly

findings.label: Pending actions
findings.value: Open items include stocking vegetables (mentioned Day 1, not confirmed done), sprouts on order as of Day 5, and building a consistent ACV habit.

risk_flags.rationale: A week of consistently short sleep (around 5-5.5 hours on several days) culminated in the client briefly falling asleep during a work meeting and reporting feeling very low, which may be linked to accumulated sleep debt and work stress rather than a single bad day.

evidence.quote: I actually slept for a few seconds
I feel I can sleep for days

risk_flags.rationale: Bloating and acidity were reported on at least four separate days without clear resolution, alongside inconsistent protein intake, which may be linked but was never established as causal in the conversation.

evidence.quote: Bloating is back and I feel like I have gained weight

risk_flags.flag: Meal planning disrupted by school schedule
risk_flags.rationale: The client explicitly named her school-related schedule as a recurring obstacle to consistent meal planning.

recommended_next_action.action: Schedule a short live check-in (call, not just async messages) focused specifically on sleep load, work stress, and realistic meal-planning logistics around the school schedule.
recommended_next_action.rationale: The Day 7 exhaustion episode and the recurring bloating pattern are the kind of findings that benefit from a real conversation rather than continued asynchronous tracking, since the underlying causes (stress, time constraints) haven't surfaced clearly through daily text updates alone.
